summary: Frontend-only visual regression test for form styling changes. Verified all admin dialogs now use compact h-9 (36px) inputs. Login and ForgotPassword pages unaffected by the change. Dialog animations confirmed in CSS.

login_page.detail: Login page inputs use .auth-input CSS class → height=48px (not affected by input.jsx h-9 change)

forgot_password_page.detail: ForgotPassword inputs have explicit h-11 class → renders at 44px. All 4 inputs (email, reset-code, new-password, confirm-password) verified with h-11.

create_customer_dialog.detail: All 11 inputs render at 36px (h-9). Pill-shaped (rounded-full) with uppercase labels, gap-4 spacing. Select triggers also h-9.

edit_customer_dialog.detail: All inputs render at 36px (h-9), matching Create Customer styling.

create_user_dialog.detail: 3 inputs (Full Name, Email, Temporary Password) all h-9 (36px). Role select also compact.

products_new_page.detail: Product Name input h-9 (36px). Category and Page Layout SelectTriggers both h-9 (36px). Smaller than previous h-11.

new_resource_dialog.detail: Title and Slug/URL inputs h-9 (36px). Category select trigger h-9 (36px). Dialog is compact and clean.

dialog_animation.detail: dialogIn @keyframes defined in index.css (scale(0.96) translateY(8px) → scale(1) translateY(0)), applied to [role='dialog'] > div and [data-radix-dialog-content]. focusGlow enhanced. Animation visible in screenshots.

critical_code_review_comments: All form styling changes verified: input.jsx h-11→h-9, select.jsx h-11→h-9, UniversalFormRenderer pillInput/pillSelect compact-aware, AddressFieldRenderer compact-aware
ForgotPassword correctly overrides with explicit h-11 to maintain auth page sizing
Login page continues using .auth-input CSS class (48px) - completely unaffected by input.jsx change
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 270: Verified form styling changes. All admin dialogs (Create/Edit Customer, Create User, New Resource, Products) use h-9 (36px) inputs. Auth pages (Login=48px, ForgotPassword=44px with h-11) unaffected. Dialog animations (dialogIn keyframe) confirmed in CSS. No issues found.
